title: Deliciously Dense Middle Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Middle Eastern dish featuring lamb, raspberry jam, and lardons, perfect for a special date night, this recipe is also anti-inflammatory friendly and made in a kadhai vessel.
instructions: Heat the kadhai vessel over medium heat and add the rendered lardons to cook until crispy.
Remove the lardons from the kadhai and set aside, leaving the drippings in the vessel.
Add the chopped onion to the kadhai and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the kadhai and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb pieces to the kadhai and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the kadhai and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth, raspberry jam, and cooked lardons to the kadhai, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
